Q2 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(i) In Von-Neumann architecture, program is always required in the main-memory.
(ii) In Harvard architecture, program is predefined in the code memory (ROM) and data is stored
in the data memory (RAM) separately.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The von Neumann architecture uses a single memory space for both instructions and data whereas
in Harvard architecture, there are separate buses for both instruction and data. Hence, both the
statements are correct.

Q3 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Among Hardwired, horizontal and vertical micro-programmed control architecture, which is the fastest?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
A hardwired control unit is faster because it directly generates control signals using hardware,
while the micro-programmed control unit is slower because of the time it takes to fetch
microinstructions from the control memory.
Speed : Hardwired architecture > Horizontal micro-programmed architecture > Vertical
micro-programmed architecture

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which one of the following is NOT a hazard in pipelining?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• Standard hazards are structural, data and control hazard. “Capacity hazard” is not a type of
pipeline hazard.
• Structural hazards occur when two or more instructions simultaneously require the same
hardware resources.
• Data Hazard occurs when an instruction depends on the result of a previous instruction that
has not yet completed.
• A control hazard, often referred to as a branch hazard, happens when the pipeline incorrectly
predicts a branch, leading to unnecessary instructions.

Q6 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following are the characteristics of the deadlock?
1. Mutual exclusion
2. Hold and wait
3. No pre-emption
4. Circular wait

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Deadlock is a situation where the execution of two or more processes is blocked because each
process holds some resource and waits for another resource held by some other process. The
necessary conditions for deadlock to occur are:
• Mutual Exclusion: There must exist at least one resource in the system which can be used by
only one process at a time.
• Hold and Wait: There must exist a process which holds some resource and waits for another
resource held by some other process.
• No pre-emption: A resource cannot be preempted from a process by force. A process can only
release a resource voluntarily.
• Circular Wait: A process is waiting for the resource held by the second process, which is
waiting for the resource held by the third process and so on, till the last process is waiting for
a resource held by the first process. This forms a circular chain.

Q7 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following is not a type of machine instruction?
1. Data transfer instruction.
2. Data manipulation instruction.
3. Program control instruction.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Types of machine instructions:
1. Data transfer instruction: MOVE, LOAD, STORE, etc.
2. Data manipulation instruction (computation): Arithmetic, logical, shift and rotate instructions.
3. Program control instruction: JUMP, CALL, etc.

Q8 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Microprogrammed control unit uses fixed logic to interpret instructions.
S2 : Horizontal microprogramming needs signal decoders as like vertical microprogramming.
Which of the following options is correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Hardwired control unit uses fixed logic circuits to interpret instructions and generate appropriate
control signals. Micro-programmed control unit is flexible in nature as a code is used to generate
the control signals.
Since, the control signals in horizontal microprogram control unit are not encoded, 1 bit is required
per control signal. Hence, no signal decoder is needed unlike vertical microprogramming which
uses encoded control signals.

Q12 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A 5 stage pipeline with the stages taking 1, 1, 3, 1, 1 units of time has a throughput of ________ instructions per unit time. (rounded upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 0.33
Solution

0.33 (0.30 - 0.40)

(Execution Time)pipeline = max (1,1,3,1,1) = 3 units. On average, one instruction is completed every
3 units of time, hence, resulting in a throughput of 1/3 = 0.33 instructions per time unit.

Q15 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following scheduling algorithms may cause starvation??

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, c)
In SJF, SRTF starration may occur. In the shortest remaining time first scheduling algorithm, the
process with the shortest remaining time gets the CPU next. However, if short processes keep
coming, a long process may never get the CPU, causing starvation. Also, in shortest job first, if
new, shorter jobs are consistently arriving, a long-running job might never get the CPU, leading
to starvation.

Q16 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, d)
• Starvation occurs when a low-priority process keeps waiting indefinitely because higherpriority
processes continuously get the CPU.
• The convoy effect in First-Come-First-Serve (FCFS) scheduling arises when shorter processes
are delayed by longer ones in the queue.
• First-Come, First-Serve (FCFS) scheduling guarantees that every process will eventually be
served in the order it arrives, ensuring no process is starved.
Hence, options (a), (b) and (d) are correct.

Q18 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Vertical microprogrammed organisation is highly encoded schemes that use compact codes to
specify only a small number of control functions in each micro-instruction.
2. Hardwired control unit are faster than microprogrammed ones. They are used in RISCs.
3. Microprogram is also called as firmware.
Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• In Vertical micro-programmed control unit, the control signals are represented in the encoded
binary format. Here, ‘n’ control signals require log2n bits.
• Hardwired control unit generate control signals directly using dedicated logic circuits, rather than
relying on microcode stored in memory. Hence, they are faster than microprogrammed control
units. RISC processors, with their simpler instruction format, typically use hardwired control units.
• A microprogram consisting of the set of microinstructions is often referred to as firmware.

Q22 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

With a single resource, deadlock occurs

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Deadlock is a situation where the execution of two or more processes is blocked because each
process holds some resource and waits for another resource held by some other process. For a
single resource, the necessary conditions of hold and wait and circular wait are not met. Therefore,
deadlock will never occur for a single resource.

Q23 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A compiler is

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
A compiler is a software program that accepts a program written in a high level language and
generate object code (machine code). Thereafter, a linker links several object files to generate an
executable file.

Q24 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are correct regarding CISC?
1. Programs in CISC architecture take less space in memory.
2. It is used in personal applications.
3. It uses microprogrammed CPU.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• CISC architecture aims to reduce the number of instructions per program while increasing the
number of cycles per instruction. Hence, programs in CISC architecture takes less space in
memory.
• RISC is used in high-end applications like video processing, telecommunications, and image
processing whereas CISC is used in low-end applications such as personal applications.
• CISC processor uses microprogrammed control unit as compared to hardwired control unit
used in RISC.

Q33 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements about floating-point representation are true?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, c, d)
• In the single-precision IEEE 754 format, the exponent is stored using a bias value of 127. The
actual exponent E is calculated as:
E = Stored exponent-127
• Denormalized numbers are used to represent values closer to zero. Denormalized (or
subnormal) numbers are represented with a biased exponent of all 0 bits, which represents an
exponent of –126.
• For normalized number in IEEE 754, the mantissa (fraction) always has an implicit leading 1.
e.g. if the mantissa is stored as xxxxx, it is interpreted as 1.xxxxx
• The sign bit in IEEE 754 represents the sign of the number, with 0 indicating positive and 1 indicating negative number.
Thus, all the options are correct.
